title: Design Notes
text: estimate_interaction() rewritten around three rules. Identity, never position: exposure must match tidy term exactly; level’s interaction coefficient resolves exact keys (exposure:interactionLevel, either variable order, bare exposure:interaction numerically-coded modifier); variance-covariance matrix indexed coefficient name — retiring grep()[1] positions. Levels generalize: one row per level attached-data factor (binary categorical), reference level carrying exposure coefficient alone level j adding interaction coefficient var(b_e) + var(b_j) + 2cov (Figueiras et al. 1998), critical values recorded residual df (falling normal family records none). One across-levels p-value: coefficient’s test single interaction coefficient (preserving binary behavior), joint Wald chi-square several. add_interaction() "interaction" layout come pair — block defines preset’s rows, errors without , frame built dedicated realization (realize_interaction(), single outcome × exposure launch, exponentiation deferred estimates block / M5 inference everywhere). frame, per-level statistics ordinary rows interaction p-value first shipped group-scoped cell (row_scope = "group"), exercising M6.6 rowspan emulation real. old forest table exactly chain "interaction" layout + add_n() + add_estimates() + add_forest(); verified, tbl_interaction_forest() deleted M6.7 terms (outright, pre-release), taking gt-forest.R, skipped private-data test, now-unused tbls shared-argument docs — gt.R keeps theme_gt_compact().

what: Adjustment, mediation, and the total-effect / direct-effect distinction
title: 6. The Causal Reasoning
text: consequential place role vocabulary shapes actual output mediator role. Adjusting common cause exposure outcome (confounder) adjusting variable causal pathway (mediator) “adding covariate” ordinary regression syntax — answer completely different questions. Conditioning mediator blocks part effect study. VanderWeele Robins’s work mediation (see also VanderWeele’s 2015 text, Explanation Causal Inference) formalizes decomposing total effect direct effect (exposure’s effect mediator) indirect effect (effect travels ). term wrapped .m() triggers exactly decomposition fmls() (vignette("formulas")): instead one adjusted formula, family expands triad — total effect mediation pathway held open, exposure’s effect mediator, mediator’s effect outcome exposure’s presence. Comparing exposure’s coefficient across first third formulas lets analyst judge much total effect travels mediator, rather accidentally reporting direct effect believing total one.
code: fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + .m(qsec) + hp) |> formula() #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + qsec + hp #>  #> [[2]] #> qsec ~ wt #>  #> [[3]] #> mpg ~ wt + qsec

what: Effect modification is a property of the exposure’s effect, not a coefficient
title: 6. The Causal Reasoning
text: VanderWeele Robins (2007) distinguish four types effect modification based modifying variable sits causal DAG relative exposure outcome — distinction matters “interaction term’s p-value significant” much weaker claim “exposure’s effect differs meaningfully across levels modifier.” mesa‘s interaction role (.()) estimate_interaction() built around second framing. Rather reporting interaction coefficient — describes difference difference, effect either subgroup experiences — estimate_interaction() derives exposure’s effect within level modifier, fitted coefficients’ variance-covariance structure (Figueiras, Domenech-Massons, Cadarso 1998): within reference level effect exposure’s coefficient; within level j coefficient plus level’s interaction term, variance var(b_exposure) + var(b_j) + 2 cov(b_exposure, b_j). number clinician policymaker can act — “effect men” “effect women” — raw interaction coefficient .

title: 4. Fitting and Model Tables

title: 2. Formulas and Patterns

code: f <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "sequential") f #> <fmls: 3 formulas> #>   outcome: mpg #>   exposure: wt #> mpg ~ wt #> mpg ~ wt + hp #> mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl formula(f) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp #>  #> [[3]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl

title: 2. Formulas and Patterns
text: Assuming wt, hp, cyl covariates alongside exposure wt (double duty exposure pattern input), four built-patterns answer “many formulas, ’s ”: direct (default): every covariate , one formula. sequential: covariates enter one time, formula nesting last — classic “unadjusted, progressively adjusted” table. parallel: one formula per covariate, paired exposure — useful screening many candidate adjustors independently. fundamental: every right-hand term, exposure included, entirely alone unadjusted — one formula per term, univariate screen patterns build outward . formula_patterns() lists every pattern currently available — four built-ins plus anything registered:
code: formula(fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "direct")) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl formula(fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "parallel")) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ wt + cyl formula(fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "fundamental")) #> Using `fundamental` decomposition pattern:  #> - Mediation term: NA #> - Stratifying term: NA #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ hp #>  #> [[3]] #> mpg ~ cyl formula_patterns() #> [1] "direct"      "fundamental" "parallel"    "sequential"

title: 2. Formulas and Patterns
text: term wrapped .() candidate effect modifier. crossed exposure automatically, pair grouped travels pattern together:
code: fi <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + .i(am)) #> Interaction term `am` was applied to exposure term `wt` formula(fi) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + am + wt:am

title: 2. Formulas and Patterns
text: term wrapped .m() just adjusted — presence asks causal question, fmls() builds three formulas epidemiologist needs reason mediation (VanderWeele Robins 2007), instead one .x()/.c() produce: mpg ~ wt + qsec + hp — exposure’s effect pathway mediator held open (adjusted mediator). qsec ~ wt — exposure’s effect mediator . mpg ~ wt + qsec — mediator’s effect outcome exposure’s presence. Comparing exposure’s estimate across (1) (3) lets judge much total effect travels mediator.
code: fm <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + .m(qsec) + hp) fm #> <fmls: 3 formulas> #>   outcome: mpg #>   exposure: wt #>   mediator: qsec #> mpg ~ wt + qsec + hp #> qsec ~ wt #> mpg ~ wt + qsec formula(fm) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + qsec + hp #>  #> [[2]] #> qsec ~ wt #>  #> [[3]] #> mpg ~ wt + qsec

what: Grouped terms move as one block
title: 2. Formulas and Patterns
text: Terms always enter leave formula together — set dummy-coded categories one variable, say — tagged .g() tier instead split apart sequential parallel pattern: hp cyl enter second formula together, one adjustment step, rather one time.
code: fg <- fmls(mpg ~ wt + .g1(hp) + .g1(cyl), pattern = "sequential") formula(fg) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl

title: 2. Formulas and Patterns
text: Two families merge c() (vctrs::vec_c()); term arrives sides conflicting definitions, left-definition wins message names resolution — never silent overwrite.
code: f1 <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp) f2 <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + cyl) formula(c(f1, f2)) #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt + hp #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ wt + cyl

code: unadjusted <- function(x) {   apply_fundamental_pattern(x) } register_pattern("unadjusted", unadjusted) "unadjusted" %in% formula_patterns() #> [1] TRUE  formula(fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "unadjusted")) #> Using `fundamental` decomposition pattern:  #> - Mediation term: NA #> - Stratifying term: NA #> [[1]] #> mpg ~ wt #>  #> [[2]] #> mpg ~ hp #>  #> [[3]] #> mpg ~ cyl

what: A bare mesa is already a table
title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: mesa() deliberately bare — takes selection arguments — puts everything fitted mesa default labels: Printing <mesa> specification shows ’s without rendering anything: layout, current selection, column blocks far. as_gt() renders — bare specification already produces minimal table, estimate interval every displayed term:
code: mt |> mesa() #> <mesa> specification: 3 fitted models × lm #>   data: d #>   layout: adjustment (rows: adjustment sets, groups: outcomes) #>   selection: everything fitted (bare mesa) #>   columns: estimate + CI (default) #>  #> # `as_gt()` renders; `select_*()` / `modify_labels()` refine the mesa mt |> mesa() |> as_gt()

what: Growing the table, one verb at a time
title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: Nothing decided front. verb narrows adds exactly one thing, verbs compose order: select_outcomes() / select_exposures() / select_terms() / select_adjustment() / select_strata() narrow shown, input labeled formula — selecting labeling one gesture. modify_labels() relabels terms, levels, columns late, without reselecting anything. add_estimates(), add_n(), add_events(), add_rate_difference(), add_forest(), add_interaction() append one column block. modify_layout(), modify_style() assign table’s axes accents, digits, padding. <mesa> specification declarative — verbs record instructions, resolution happens as_gt() — repeating verb replaces just instruction, message, rather compounding silently. Rethinking label, narrowing selection , always cheap.
code: mt |>   mesa() |>   select_adjustment(1 ~ "Unadjusted", 3 ~ "Fully adjusted") |>   add_estimates(columns = list(beta ~ "Estimate", conf ~ "95% CI")) |>   modify_labels(wt ~ "Weight (1000 lbs)") |>   as_gt()

title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: layout preset complete assignment grammar’s four axes (rows, row groups, columns, spanners), selected modify_layout().

title: 5. Making the Mesa

title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: Event counts incidence rates, adjusted-estimate rows, term levels columns — shape hazard-ratio table: add_events() reads follow-column Surv() outcome can (followup = time makes explicit; optional data’s follow-column matches outcome’s time argument). Cox model’s estimates come back exponentiated default (family inference vignette("fitting")), column already labeled HR correct scale.
code: lung <- survival::lung lung$sex <- factor(lung$sex, levels = 1:2, labels = c("Male", "Female"))  mtSurv <-   fmls(Surv(time, status) ~ .x(sex) + age, pattern = "sequential") |>   fit(.fn = survival::coxph, data = lung, raw = FALSE) |>   model_table(data = lung)  mtSurv |>   mesa() |>   modify_layout(preset = "levels") |>   select_adjustment(1 ~ "Unadjusted", 2 ~ "Age-adjusted") |>   add_events(followup = time) |>   add_rate_difference() |>   add_estimates(columns = list(beta ~ "HR", conf ~ "95% CI")) |>   as_gt()

title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: Interaction levels rows, grouped interaction term, across-levels p-value floating band — effect modification made visible: add_interaction() implies "interaction" layout — defines rows, declaring block declaring layout; separate modify_layout(preset = "interaction") call never required common case.
code: mtInt <-   fmls(mpg ~ .x(hp) + .i(cyl)) |>   fit(.fn = lm, data = d, raw = FALSE) |>   model_table(data = d) #> Interaction term `cyl` was applied to exposure term `hp`  mtInt |>   mesa() |>   add_interaction() |>   add_n(label = "No.") |>   add_estimates(columns = list(beta ~ "Estimate", conf ~ "95% CI",                                 p ~ "P for interaction")) |>   as_gt() #> `add_interaction()` sets the layout to the `interaction` preset.

what: A forest column, on any table
title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: add_forest() appends forest-plot column table already carries estimate interval — reads computes nothing new, adding dropping never changes cell:
code: mtInt |>   mesa() |>   add_interaction() |>   add_estimates() |>   add_forest() |>   as_gt() #> `add_interaction()` sets the layout to the `interaction` preset.

title: 5. Making the Mesa
text: modify_style() emphasizes cells meet criterion — displayed statistic, just p-value — controls formatting table-wide:
code: mt |>   mesa() |>   add_estimates() |>   modify_style(accents = list(p < 0.05 ~ "bold"), digits = 3) |>   as_gt()

title: 3. Playing with Data
text: Every verb pipeable, every one returns modified fmls — compose single readable chain, way dplyr verbs . add_strata() / remove_strata() promote term (release ) strata role. stratified family fit — fit() fits one model per stratum level, automatically. add_terms() / remove_terms() add drop covariates every formula family . swap_outcome() exchanges one outcome another, keeping rest family — exposure, covariates, pattern — intact. Either old ~ new, bare replacement name family single outcome. subset_data() records data-filtering instructions — plain logical expressions data’s variables — fit() applies later, fitting family per subset. filter applied ; recorded instruction, way stratum level .
code: f <- fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp)  f |>   add_strata(am) |>   add_terms(cyl) |>   subset_data(disp > 100) #> <fmls: 1 formula> #>   outcome: mpg #>   exposure: wt #>   strata: am #>   subsets: disp > 100 #> mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl f |> swap_outcome(qsec) #> <fmls: 1 formula> #>   outcome: qsec #>   exposure: wt #> qsec ~ wt + hp

title: 3. Playing with Data

[set_data()] step family actually meets one: classifies every term’s type distribution, — detail matters strata — stamps observed levels onto categorical term, strata included. Call last, roles needs classify already assigned: deck header now reads strata: (2 levels) — single model fit, family already knows many models stratified fit produce. Calling set_data() add_strata() classify plain predictor first, strata levels appear; set_data() reflects roles exist moment runs, reads naturally last step chain structural verbs.

what: A term is more than a name
title: 1. Terms and Causal Roles
text: plain R formula knows variable’s position — left tilde right . way say wt exposure whose effect care , cyl confounder adjusting , defines subpopulations want fit separately. tm class fills gap. Printed, tm vector looks almost like formula came — every element already carries role (, everything mpg defaulted predictor, mpg inferred outcome position left-hand side).
code: t <- tm(mpg ~ wt + hp) t #> <term[3]> #> mpg #> wt #> hp

title: 1. Terms and Causal Roles
text: Roles causal, first-class: term’s role knowable, updatable, visibly changes happens downstream — formulas expand ([fmls()]), models fit ([fit()]), results labeled reach table ([mesa()]). role applied wrapping variable shortcut, right inside formula: hp left bare defaulted predictor — role assumed right-hand term asserted causal position, one pattern rotates adjusts . Nothing permanent: roles updated fact [update.tm()], just like label .
code: term_roles() #> $outcome #> [1] ".o" #>  #> $exposure #> [1] ".x" #>  #> $predictor #> [1] ".p" #>  #> $confounder #> [1] ".c" #>  #> $mediator #> [1] ".m" #>  #> $strata #> [1] ".s" #>  #> $interaction #> [1] ".i" #>  #> $random #> [1] ".r" t <- tm(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + .c(cyl)) t #> <term[4]> #> mpg #> wt #> hp #> cyl describe(t, "role") #> $mpg #> [1] "outcome" #>  #> $wt #> [1] "exposure" #>  #> $hp #> [1] "predictor" #>  #> $cyl #> [1] "confounder" t2 <- update(t, role = hp ~ "confounder") describe(t2, "role") #> $mpg #> [1] "outcome" #>  #> $wt #> [1] "exposure" #>  #> $hp #> [1] "confounder" #>  #> $cyl #> [1] "confounder"

title: 1. Terms and Causal Roles
text: Wrapping term recognized transformation — log(), sqrt(), scale(), factor(), ordered(), polynomial spline markers — keeps full call term’s name, formula rebuilds exactly, without mesa needing re-apply anything . wrapper additionally recorded downstream interpretation. term_transformations() lists every wrapper mesa recognizes; unrecognized call (survival::Surv(time, status), say) carried whole opaque term rather rejected.
code: t3 <- tm(mpg ~ .x(log(wt)) + hp) t3 #> <term[3]> #> mpg #> log(wt) #> hp describe(t3, "transformation") #> $mpg #> [1] NA #>  #> $`log(wt)` #> [1] "log" #>  #> $hp #> [1] NA formula(t3) #> mpg ~ log(wt) + hp

code: # The "adjustment" chain — adjustment sets on rows, outcomes as row # groups, a statistic block per term (the retired `tbl_beta()` shape) d <- mtcars d$cyl <- factor(d$cyl) mt <-   fmls(mpg ~ .x(wt) + hp + cyl, pattern = "sequential") |>   fit(.fn = lm, data = d, raw = FALSE) |>   model_table(data = d) mt |>   mesa() |>   select_adjustment(1 ~ "Unadjusted", 3 ~ "Fully adjusted") |>   add_estimates(columns = list(beta ~ "Estimate", conf ~ "95% CI")) |>   modify_labels(wt ~ "Weight (1000 lbs)") |>   as_gt()                     Weight (1000 lbs)            Estimate (95% CI)     mpg     Unadjusted -5.34 (-6.49, -4.20)Fully adjusted -3.18 (-4.66, -1.70) # The "levels" chain — event and rate rows over adjusted estimates, term # levels on columns (the retired hazard-table shape) lung <- survival::lung lung$sex <- factor(lung$sex, levels = 1:2, labels = c("Male", "Female")) mt <-   fmls(Surv(time, status) ~ .x(sex) + age, pattern = "sequential") |>   fit(.fn = survival::coxph, data = lung, raw = FALSE) |>   model_table(data = lung) mt |>   mesa() |>   modify_layout(preset = "levels") |>   select_adjustment(1 ~ "Unadjusted", 2 ~ "Age-adjusted") |>   add_events(followup = time) |>   add_rate_difference() |>   add_estimates(columns = list(beta ~ "HR", conf ~ "95% CI")) |>   as_gt()                     sex            Male       Female       Rate difference (95% CI)     Surv(time, status)     Events 112 53 Rate per 100 person-years 104.7 63.5 -41.2 (-67.0, -15.4)Unadjusted  0.59 (0.42, 0.82) Age-adjusted  0.60 (0.43, 0.83)
